text.title: Agency Cashflow Management Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Agency Cashflow Management` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every new engagement MUST require a non-refundable deposit ≥ 30% of contract value before kickoff. Deposit floor below this voids the engagement at sales review.
rule.rationale: Deposits below 30% leave the agency funding the engagement; floor is the single most reliable runway protection.

rule.statement: Milestone invoices MUST issue within 24h of acceptance and carry net-7 terms with named approver per milestone. No bundled-end invoicing.
rule.rationale: Bundled invoicing balloons AR-age; per-milestone net-7 keeps AR inside the contractor-payable window.

rule.statement: Contractor outflow dates MUST be mapped against expected client inflow dates in the artefact, with a documented bridge action (line of credit, deferred contractor terms, deposit acceleration) when the gap exceeds 30 days.
rule.rationale: Un-bridged gaps cause emergency credit-card runs; explicit mapping forces the bridge action to be chosen before crisis.

rule.statement: Late-payment escalation MUST be a tiered policy (day-1 reminder, day-7 manager call, day-14 work-stop, day-30 collections) with a single named owner per tier. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc collections take 60-90 days; tiered escalation lands payment in 14-21 days on average.

rule.statement: The artefact MUST contain a rolling 60-day cashflow forecast (weekly granularity) reviewed weekly by the named owner, with variance log per row when actual diverges by > 15%.
rule.rationale: Forecasts beyond 60 days hallucinate inflows; weekly review with variance-log keeps the forecast honest.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
